title: MagratheaPHP::AddCodeFolder()
sub: Adds one or more code folders for the autoloader.

title: MagratheaPHP::AddFeature()
sub: Adds feature folders based on the application root. For each feature, it adds `features/[name]` and `features/[name]/Base`.

title: MagratheaPHP::AppNamespace()
sub: Sets the project's root namespace, used to scope autoloader errors. Only classes within this namespace will trigger a MagratheaException when not found.

title: MagratheaPHP::AppPath()
sub: Sets the application's root path. Also defines the `magRoot` as the parent directory.

title: MagratheaPHP::AppVersion()
sub: Gets the version of the application from the `version` file in the project root.

title: MagratheaPHP::Connect()
sub: Connects to the database using credentials from the configuration.

title: MagratheaPHP::Debug()
sub: Enables the debugger. This will also log database queries.

title: MagratheaPHP::Dev()
sub: Sets the environment to development mode. Enables error reporting and display.

title: MagratheaPHP::GetAppRoot()
sub: Returns the root path of the application.

title: MagratheaPHP::GetConfigRoot()
sub: Gets the path to the configuration directory.

title: MagratheaPHP::GetDB()
sub: Gets the singleton instance of the Database object.

title: MagratheaPHP::GetMagratheaRoot()
sub: Gets the root directory of the MagratheaPHP library.

title: MagratheaPHP::MinVersion()
sub: Checks if the current Magrathea version meets a minimum requirement. Otherwise, it displays an error message.

title: MagratheaPHP::Prod()
sub: Sets the environment to production mode. Errors will be sent to the logger instead of being displayed.

title: MagratheaPHP::StartDB()
sub: Connects to the database. Alias for `Connect()`.

title: MagratheaPHP::StartSession()
sub: Starts a PHP session if one is not already active.

title: MagratheaPHP::GetDocumentationLink()
sub: Returns the link to Magrathea's official documentation.

title: MagratheaPHP::Test()
sub: A simple test function to check if MagratheaPHP is working.

title: Config::GetAvailableEnvironments()
sub: returns an array with available environments

title: Config::GetConfig()
sub: `$config_name` can be called to get a parameter from inside a section of the config file. To achieve this, you should use a slash (/) to separate the section from the property. If the slash is not used, the function will return the property only if it's on the root. If `$config_name` is a section name, the function will return the full section as an Array. If `$config_name` is empty, the function will return the full config as an Array (**not recommended!**). If config variable name starts with $=, it will be interpreted as an environment variable and its value will be returned.

title: Config::GetConfigFromDefault()
sub: This function will get the $config_name property from `magrathea.conf`. It will get from the section defined on `general/use_environment`.

title: Config::GetConfigSection()
sub: `$section_name` is the name of the section that will be returned as an array.

title: Config::GetEnvironment()
sub: This function will return the environment being used in the project. The environment is defined in `general/use_environment` property and can be defined in the `magrathea.conf` file.

title: Config::SetEnvironment()
sub: This function will set the environment for future operations

title: ConfigFile::CreateFileIfNotExists()
sub: Creates the configuration file if it doesn't exists. And saves it.

title: ConfigFile::GetConfig()
sub: Gets configuration If an acceptable config name, returns its value @todo 	exception 704 on key does not exists

title: ConfigFile::GetConfigSection()
sub: Gets a full config section @todo 	exception 704 on key does not exists

title: ConfigFile::SetFile()
sub: Sets the name of the config file

title: ConfigFile::SetPath()
sub: Set the path to the confi file

title: MagratheaHelper::HexToRgb()
sub: Converts HEXrgb to DECrgb (#CB8008) to [r:203, g:128, b:8]

title: Database::ImportFile()
sub: imports a .sql file to the database

title: Database::PrepareAndExecute()
sub: Prepares and execute a query and returns the inserted id (if any) @todo validates types and avoids injection. Does it?

title: Database::Query()
sub: executes the query and returns the full data

title: Database::QueryAll()
sub: executes the query and returns the full data in an array

title: Database::QueryMulti()
sub: receives a string with multiple queries and executes them all

title: Database::QueryOne()
sub: executes the query and returns only the first value of the first row of the result

title: Database::QueryRow()
sub: executes the query and returns only the first row of the result

title: Database::QueryTransaction()
sub: receives an array of queries and executes them all

title: Database::SetConnectionArray()
sub: Sets the connection array object array( 'hostspec' => $host, 'database' => $database, 'username' => $username, 'password' => $password, );

title: Database::SetFetchMode()
sub: Sets fetchmode, according with MDB2 values. Default mode: assoc. options available: assoc: array with keys as the column names object: object with columns as properties if anything different from those values is sent, "assoc" is used

title: Query::CountSQL()
sub: How many? Tell me the amount!!! We get all the information that you sent to the function and, instead of returning the results, we count how many rows you will have back This will return the query for the count.

title: Query::Fields()
sub: Fields to be included on the query

title: Query::GetJoins()
sub: all the joins that were built in this query

title: Query::GetObjArray()
sub: all the objects that are used in this query

title: Query::Join()
sub: A Join to be included in the query

title: Query::Limit()
sub: Let's put a limit on it to help our database? Yes!

title: Query::Obj()
sub: Set object for getting information in query

title: Query::Page()
sub: Which page? working altogether with *Limit*, to bring a specific page, with a specific amount of results

title: Query::SQL()
sub: ...and we're gonna build the query for you. After gathering all the information, this function returns to you a wonderful SQL query for be executed or to be hang in the wall of a gallery art exhibition, depending how good you are in building queries

title: Query::SelectExtra()
sub: Includes a field to select query to be added in the end of the clause

title: Query::SelectObj()
sub: Selects all the fields for an object

title: Query::W()
sub: Builds where, receiving the column and the value

title: Query::Where()
sub: Builds where! Is possible to send a string or an array, where the keys of the array will be the name of the fields which the query will be done

title: Query::WhereArray()
sub: Builds where Same as *Where*, but accepting only array

title: Query::BuildSelect()
sub: *INTERNAL USE* gets an array with "fields" and returns it with "table.fields" sample: array_walk($joinObjDbValues, 'BuildSelect', $joinObjTable);

title: Query::BuildWhere()
sub: *INTERNAL USE* Build *Where* clause with information sent

title: Query::Clean()
sub: Cleans a value in order to avoid SQL injection

title: Query::Create()
sub: Creates. Just that. Just like God did.

title: Query::Select()
sub: Generates a SELECT query in a query SELECT [blablabla] FROM ... the [blablabla] should be sent to this function. Got it?

title: Query::SplitArrayResult()
sub: *INTERNAL USE* Gets the result and splits into its specific array for each object

title: MagratheaModel::Assign()

title: MagratheaModel::GetById()
sub: Returns object by Id. If null, creates a null instance of the object. This will also load any related objects that are set as "autoload" internally. if an object with the given id can not be found, or any of the auto-load related objects can not be found an exception will be thrown.

title: MagratheaModel::GetDbValues()
sub: Gets array of table column values

title: MagratheaModel::GetFieldsForSelect()
sub: Prepare fields for this model for a select statement

sub: Gets the next auto increment id for this object

sub: Checks if the object exists (id not null)

title: MagratheaModel::LoadObjectFromTableRow()
sub: Receives an array with the columns and values and associates then internally into the object

title: MagratheaModel::ModelName()
sub: returns the name of the class without the namespace

title: MagratheaModel::Ref()
sub: returns a string for identifying the object in a relation

title: MagratheaModel::Save()
sub: Saves: Using a insert if pk is not set and an update if pk is set Basically, Inserts if id does not exists and updates if id does exists

sub: Include all classes presents on `Models` folder

title: MagratheaModelControl::Count()
sub: Runs a Magrathea Query and returns the count of matching rows

title: MagratheaModelControl::GetMultipleObjects()
sub: This function allows to build a query getting multiple objects at once

title: MagratheaModelControl::GetPagination()
sub: Runs query with Pagination and returns a MagratheaPagination object, ready to be returned directly from an API controller (MagratheaApi::ReturnSuccess() recognizes it). By default ($withTotal=false), avoids the extra COUNT(*) query: it fetches one row more than requested to determine `has_more`, then trims the result back to $limit.

title: MagratheaModelControl::GetRowWhere()
sub: Builds query with where clause, returning only first row

title: MagratheaModelControl::QueryOne()
sub: Runs a query and returns the first result

title: MagratheaModelControl::QueryResult()
sub: Runs a query and returns the result

title: MagratheaModelControl::QueryRow()
sub: Runs a query and returns the first row of result

title: MagratheaModelControl::Run()
sub: Runs a Magrathea Query and returns a list of objects

title: MagratheaModelControl::RunMagQuery()
sub: Runs a Magrathea Query and returns a list of objects (calls Run function)

title: MagratheaModelControl::RunPagination()
sub: Runs query with Pagination. This way, is not necessary to worry about including pagination on Magrathea Query, this function can deal with it

title: MagratheaModelControl::RunQuery()
sub: Run a query and return a list of the objects

title: MagratheaModelControl::RunRow()
sub: Run a query and return the first object available

title: MagratheaModelControl::ShowAll()
sub: Show all elements from an object

title: MagratheaApi::Add()

sub: Starts the API, processes the request, and returns the response.

sub: Adds a query to the debug

sub: After temporarily setting a debugging type, gets it back to what it was

sub: Adds an error to the debugger

sub: Returns the debug type in a descritive format

title: Debugger::SetLogFile()
sub: Set the name of log file that will be used (it will be created inside "*logs*" folder)

title: Debugger::SetTemp()
sub: Sets a debugger type temporarily (usefull when need to check a specific operation, for example)

title: Debugger::SetType()
sub: Sets the debugger method It can be: *DEV*, *DEBUG*, *LOG*, *NONE* => **Debugger::DEV** = Will print the debugs as it appears in the code **Debugger::DEBUG** = Will store all the debugs and print it later **Debugger::LOG** = Will log queries and other debugs in the code **Debugger::ANALYSIS** = Will return the error with the API **Debugger::NONE** = Well... nothing to do, heh? Default: **LOG**
